steps.title: Identify what the student's claim requires
steps.content:
The student claims that some modern irrigation methods have a lineage that long predates Lake Sabrina's completion in 1908. Any supporting quotation must show that techniques in use today trace back to a time well before 1908.
  • Choice A — 'Sprinkler irrigation systems... late 20th century...': Describes a modern technique developed after 1908 — establishes no pre-1908 lineage. ✗
  • Choice B — 'importance of irrigation infrastructure today...': Stresses present-day significance only, with no historical depth. ✗
  • Choice C — 'decades following the Second World War...': References mid-20th-century growth — still after 1908, so no pre-1908 lineage. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Hohokam people... 7th century CE... design principles still utilized today': Explicitly links a 7th-century system to principles engineers use now — directly proves the long pre-1908 lineage. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Only choice D provides evidence that current irrigation methods descend from techniques centuries older than 1908. That matches choice D — D is correct.
